verses.text: In the seventh month the people gather as one to Jerusalem, and Jeshua and Zerubbabel build the altar to offer burnt offerings, for fear of the surrounding peoples was on them. They begin with worship, not walls. When danger presses, the wise first set up the altar and seek the God who alone can keep them.

In the seventh month the people gather as one to Jerusalem, and Jeshua and Zerubbabel build the altar to offer burnt offerings, for fear of the surrounding peoples was on them. They begin with worship, not walls. When danger presses, the wise first set up the altar and seek the God who alone can keep them.

In the seventh month the people gather as one to Jerusalem, and Jeshua and Zerubbabel build the altar to offer burnt offerings, for fear of the surrounding peoples was on them. They begin with worship, not walls. When danger presses, the wise first set up the altar and seek the God who alone can keep them.

They keep the feast of tabernacles and resume the daily offerings, though the temple's foundation was not yet laid. Worship does not wait for a finished building. The people give God His due at once, beginning the duty even before they have the comfort of a house.

They keep the feast of tabernacles and resume the daily offerings, though the temple's foundation was not yet laid. Worship does not wait for a finished building. The people give God His due at once, beginning the duty even before they have the comfort of a house.

They keep the feast of tabernacles and resume the daily offerings, though the temple's foundation was not yet laid. Worship does not wait for a finished building. The people give God His due at once, beginning the duty even before they have the comfort of a house.

They hire masons and carpenters, bring cedar from Tyre as Cyrus allowed, and appoint the Levites to oversee the work. The good work is set in motion with order and care. God's house is built by willing hands working together under faithful oversight.

They hire masons and carpenters, bring cedar from Tyre as Cyrus allowed, and appoint the Levites to oversee the work. The good work is set in motion with order and care. God's house is built by willing hands working together under faithful oversight.

They hire masons and carpenters, bring cedar from Tyre as Cyrus allowed, and appoint the Levites to oversee the work. The good work is set in motion with order and care. God's house is built by willing hands working together under faithful oversight.

When the foundation is laid, the priests and Levites praise the LORD with trumpets and song, 'for he is good; for his mercy endureth for ever toward Israel.' The work's first stones call forth worship. Every step of God's reviving is met with thankful praise of His enduring mercy.

When the foundation is laid, the priests and Levites praise the LORD with trumpets and song, 'for he is good; for his mercy endureth for ever toward Israel.' The work's first stones call forth worship. Every step of God's reviving is met with thankful praise of His enduring mercy.

Old men who had seen the first temple weep aloud, while many shout for joy, until the two sounds cannot be told apart. Memory mingles grief with gladness. God's present mercies are real even when they fall short of former glory; faith can rejoice while it weeps.
